ops.title: lifeproto codegen: one schema, exact-break contract, hand-rolled Swift JSON
ops.body:
- gen/spec.go is the single source of truth for the lifeproto lifecycle envelope: the protocol version, the op strings, and every message's flat field set. gen/main.go emits both bindings — wire/lifeproto/lifeproto.go (through gofmt) and Sources/DaemonKit/LifecycleWire.swift — via `go generate ./wire/...`.
- The wire shape is FLAT `{"v":2,"op":<op>, ...fields}` with no legacy parser, capability negotiation, or nested compatibility envelope. Any schema change is an intentional protocol break and requires incrementing the exact protocol version (v2 replaced the v1 additive-only regime — do not resurrect "additive-only" language).
- Cross-language bytes are pinned by testdata/golden.json, loaded by BOTH language test suites; CI's codegen drift gate regenerates and diffs, so any hand edit to a generated file fails CI.
- Swift encodes JSON BY HAND in wire order with Go-compatible HTML-safe string escaping: Foundation's JSONEncoder reorders keys (and .sortedKeys sorts alphabetically), so it can never reproduce Go's struct-field-order bytes. The emitted Swift is excluded from swiftformat/swiftlint and checked in as-is.
- []string wire fields nil-normalize to [] in the Go constructors so they never encode as null.
- moduleRoot walks up from the working directory to go.mod so the generator writes fixed output paths whether invoked via `go generate` (package dir) or `go run ./wire/lifeproto/gen` (module root).
- The generator-emitted doc comments live inside gen/main.go STRING LITERALS — they are code, not comments; editing them changes generated output and trips the drift gate. Deduplicating them is ccn task 40f1034 (T1).
